content:
- created commit 3625e6e with the required task-finished message and hosted evidence / validation summary
- checking the worktree again now because the previous git status was collected in parallel with the commit and may be stale
- next step is git push once the worktree is confirmed clean on the new commit

- refreshed Ralph task summaries, read choose-task rules, and inspected the only active task definition
- confirmed there are no active unblocked failing bug tasks, no blocker tags on the remaining failing task, and no higher-precedence meta-task candidate because a failing task still exists
- wrote .ralph/tasks/story-33-fully-new-docs/01-task-create-fully-new-docs-with-model-authored-writing-and-independent-setup-verification.md to .ralph/current_task.txt; next turn should execute that docs task with the required opencode-only authorship workflow and validation rules

- read prior Ralph progress and confirmed the current task path is story-33 docs task 01
- read the improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions required by the task wrapper
- next step is reading the task file itself to determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution

- read the story-33 task file and confirmed it is still a task-definition/planning state, not an execution pass
- because this is docs/manual-verification work, the task itself explicitly disables TDD for implementation, but the wrapper still requires creating a separate plan file first
- next step is to inspect existing Ralph plan conventions and the opencode skill so the plan format and workflow are consistent

- inspected Ralph plan conventions and confirmed task files store a <plan> pointer while plan files end with a literal execution gate line
- next step is reading the current operator-facing docs and runtime/build surfaces so the new docs plan is grounded in actual image and setup contracts

- discovered existing model-authored docs directories and confirmed some still contain stale cargo-based setup claims, which strengthens the need to treat current docs as fragmented/unsafe rather than authoritative
- next step is reading an existing docs-plan example plus the authoritative runtime/config/api surfaces that the new documentation plan must verify against

- collected the key operator-facing contracts: README quick starts, Makefile validation lanes, OpenAPI verify API, CLI/runtime route surfaces, and existing docs directories that contain stale cargo references
- next step is to check the story-33 task directory, record the available opencode-go model ids, and then write the required separate plan file plus the task's <plan> pointer

- confirmed available opencode-go model ids include glm-5.1, deepseek-v4-flash, and kimi-k2.6, so the execution plan can bind to exact models instead of placeholders
- next step is a quick check of task-file placement conventions for <plan> pointers before writing the new story-33 plan file and updating the task markdown

- created the required planning artifact at .ralph/tasks/story-33-fully-new-docs/01-task-create-fully-new-docs-with-model-authored-writing-and-independent-setup-verification_plans/2026-04-28-fully-new-docs-plan.md
- updated the story-33 task markdown to include the matching <plan> pointer
- the plan ends in TO BE VERIFIED, binds execution to opencode-go/glm-5.1, opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash, and opencode-go/kimi-k2.6, and explicitly calls out the registry/image ambiguity as a stop condition
- next turn should review that plan, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE only if the design still looks right, then quit immediately per Ralph rules

- read the story-33 docs task and its existing separate plan file
- confirmed the plan is still gated by TO BE VERIFIED and that the only material stop-condition called out in the plan is the published image contract ambiguity
- next step is to read the opencode skill and the authoritative image-publication sources to decide whether the plan can be promoted to NOW EXECUTE this turn

- read the opencode skill plus the authoritative publication sources to validate the docs-execution plan
- confirmed the current image contract is execution-safe: publish-images.yml pushes GHCR first, and scripts/ci/publish-quay-from-ghcr.sh mirrors existing GHCR images into Quay afterward, so GHCR can be treated as the default published-image source unless later execution evidence requires documenting Quay explicitly
- updated .ralph/tasks/story-33-fully-new-docs/01-task-create-fully-new-docs-with-model-authored-writing-and-independent-setup-verification_plans/2026-04-28-fully-new-docs-plan.md to end with NOW EXECUTE
- next turn should execute that plan exactly as written, starting with the pre-authoring factual audit and required opencode model workflow, then quit only after required checks pass or a new TO BE VERIFIED stop condition is hit

- entered execution for story-33 docs task after confirming the plan ends with NOW EXECUTE
- read the task file, execution plan, and required opencode skill; next step is Slice 1 factual audit plus exact opencode-go model verification before any docs are authored
- if interrupted now, resume by gathering authoritative image/CLI/endpoint/config evidence and recording it under a story-local artifacts directory

- completed the pre-authoring factual audit and wrote it to .ralph/tasks/story-33-fully-new-docs/artifacts/2026-04-28-pre-authoring-factual-audit.md
- confirmed the authoritative published image refs are ghcr.io/<owner>/runner-image:<git-sha> and ghcr.io/<owner>/verify-image:<git-sha>; README image names are stale and must not be propagated into the new docs
- wrote the first GLM source-pack prompt; next step is running opencode-go/glm-5.1 for the initial sole-author docs pass and capturing its file-manifest output verbatim

- GLM 5.1 completed both required passes and directly authored the docs under docs/public_image_operator_guide/
- only after the second pass did I start human verification; next step is contradiction scanning plus detailed source-backed review, using inline <comment> blocks only for issues
- after verification comments are in place, run DeepSeek V4 Flash on only the new docs directory, then run Kimi K2.6 style review and send any accepted style-only feedback back through GLM

- completed story-33 task 01: new canonical docs live under docs/public_image_operator_guide, stale model-specific doc dirs were removed, and the docs workflow was executed through GLM authoring, GLM improvement, DeepSeek independent review, Kimi style review, and final GLM style polish
- required checks passed: make check, make lint, and make test; make test-long was intentionally not run
- next step is close-out only: inspect git status, commit all changes with the task-finished message, push, and quit immediately
